context: Surfaced while writing M9F11 e2e-lead-capture Playwright specs.

bug.summary: POST /api/leads with an email body fails with 500 Failed to save lead on every request.
bug.rootCause: The route upserts with onConflict:'site_id,email'. In M7F4 the leads_site_email_unique constraint was replaced with a partial unique index (leads_site_email_unique_partial WHERE email IS NOT NULL) so phone-only leads could be NULL. PostgreSQL's ON CONFLICT cannot infer a partial unique index from a column list alone — PostgREST offers no WHERE predicate in its on_conflict header — so the upsert fails with 42P10 'there is no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification' for every email lead.
bug.impact: Every visitor who submitted an email through the widget's ask_email escalation action hit a 500. The dashboard never saw the lead. This broke VAL-ESCAL-011, VAL-ESCAL-013, VAL-CROSS-003 in production despite the unit tests passing (they mocked the upsert return value).
bug.detectedBy: e2e/lead-capture.spec.ts POST /api/leads against the real local Supabase stack.

fix: src/app/api/leads/route.ts — replaced upsert(onConflict) with insert-first + fall-back UPDATE on 23505 (partial-unique-index conflict). The email path runs INSERT; on 23505, runs UPDATE ... WHERE site_id=$1 AND email=$2. Phone-only path is still a plain INSERT (no dedupe key possible).

src/__tests__/leads-route.test.ts — extended mock chain to expose .update().eq().eq(); rewrote assertions to drive the new flow. Added tests: 23505 fallback hits UPDATE, non-conflict error returns 500, UPDATE failure returns 500. 16 tests now cover both paths.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run src/__tests__/leads-route.test.ts — 16 passed (was 14). pnpm exec playwright test e2e/lead-capture.spec.ts — 5/5 green.
